data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (Security overlay). The overlay APPLIES: this plan stands up genuine security surface — a public HTTPS review-bot endpoint (`/review/` on 443) + Gerrit HTTP 8080 / SSH 29418 behind a public EIP + DNS, an authn boundary on inbound webhooks, and multiple credentials/grants (a GitHub replication deploy key, a Gerrit bot HTTP token, an EC2 instance role reading SSM SecureStrings + KMS decrypt). Verdict: strong on the access-model and least-privilege dimensions; 3 grounded coaching findings (auth boundary + encryption-at-rest + secret-in-logs), all code-corroborated against infra/.

AFFIRMATIONS (overlay dimensions this plan satisfies):
- (c) LEAST-PRIVILEGE on new grants: strong and code-corroborated. SSM read scoped to `arn:...:parameter/rebar/prod/*` (explicitly NOT `*` — iam.tf:58-61), `kms:Decrypt` bounded by a `kms:ViaService` condition (iam.tf:63-72); single-owner role contract (S1 CREATES the role; S2/S4a only ADD scoped inline policies, never re-declare it — iam_s4a.tf); the replication deploy key is single-repo WRITE-only to GitHub and the sole ruleset bypass actor. No wildcard admin-for-convenience. IMDSv2 required (main.tf:50-54) defends the instance-role creds against SSRF theft.
- (d) SECRET SOURCE / no plaintext in IaC: SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot; enumerated param set completeness-checked (`/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` + `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`); the committed webhooks.config carries only a `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder substituted at boot (webhooks.config:23), not a literal.
- (a) Machine-to-machine surface gated: `/review/` on 443 is the only public app surface; the stdio MCP is deliberately NOT exposed over HTTP; the internal webhook hop is loopback-bound + not in the SG. The `webhooks`-plugin no-HMAC limitation is named honestly (webhooks.config:12) rather than papered over.

FINDINGS (3):

- [ ] Specify the HUMAN/admin authentication mechanism for the internet-facing Gerrit web UI/REST (:443) and SSH (:29418), or restrict the ingress so the current dev-auth is unreachable from the open internet. The plan opens 443 + 29418 to `0.0.0.0/0` (network.tf:33,54) behind a public EIP + DNS, but the ONLY configured Gerrit auth mode is `auth.type` (DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT) under which any anonymous visitor can "become ANY account" incl. Administrators — anonymous admin assumption from the whole internet, which also defeats the plan's core integrity claim (the `LLM-Review` submit-req can be self-voted by anyone who "becomes" a Service User/admin). The config itself flags this as unresolved (gerrit.config:70-80: "PoC ONLY ... do NOT make this Gerrit the sole gate on GitHub `main` while it is on"). This is the overlay's check (a): a sensitive/admin path NOT gated by the app's own auth mechanism on a reachable surface.
  - criteria: [preview]
  - location: AC1 ("reverse proxy on 443; Gerrit HTTP 8080 + SSH 29418"); infra/terraform/network.tf:33,54; infra/compose/gerrit.config:69-80
  - evidence: code fact — DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT is the ONLY configured auth mode (gerrit.config:75); network.tf opens 443+29418 to 0.0.0.0/0 with a public EIP/DNS; config comment "do NOT make this Gerrit the sole gate on GitHub main while it is on — it is NOT real authentication"; ABSENCE of any named human-auth mechanism (OAuth/OIDC/SSO/LDAP) in any AC/ADR; overlay check (a) sensitive paths use the app's own auth mechanism.
  - suggested_fix: Add an AC + ADR to S2 (or S3/S4a) pinning the human-auth posture BEFORE internet exposure: EITHER (a) set `auth.type = OAUTH` (gerrit-oauth-provider + GitHub/Google OIDC; the runbook infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md already scopes this) with anonymous write disabled; OR (b) if a real IdP is out of PoC scope, restrict the 443+29418 ingress `cidr_blocks` to a trusted operator CIDR/VPN/SG allowlist (not 0.0.0.0/0) so DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT is never reachable from the open internet. State it in the plan rather than deferring with "S3/S4a may refine".

- [ ] Specify encryption-at-rest (`encrypted = true` + KMS) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS data volume AND the instance root device, so they and their inherited DLM snapshots are encrypted at rest. AC1 / the "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet name only "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume ... with prevent_destroy + DLM daily snapshots" with NO at-rest-encryption statement. This is in-scope for overlay check (b): the volume STORES a secret — `refs/meta/config` carries the live inbound-webhook bot token — and DLM snapshots INHERIT the source volume's (currently absent) encryption. Code confirms the gap: `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) and `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) declare no `encrypted`, and a repo-wide `search infra/ "encrypted"` returns ZERO matches. (The S3 state bucket IS SSE-encrypted, so the EBS omission reads as an oversight, not a deliberate scoping decision.)
  - criteria: [preview]
  - location: Decomposition -> "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1; infra/terraform/main.tf:27-59, infra/terraform/backup.tf
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; code fact — main.tf:27-40 and 56-59 declare no `encrypted`, `search infra/ "encrypted"` -> 0 hits; ADR-0014: the bot token lives in `refs/meta/config` on this volume; overlay check (b) encryption at rest where sensitive data is stored.
  - suggested_fix: Add `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed EBS key or a project CMK) to `aws_ebs_volume.data` and `root_block_device`; add a line to S1's volume AC that encryption propagates to the DLM snapshots so the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` is encrypted at rest. One-line, no-cost.

- [ ] State a redaction posture for the bot token that travels in the URL query string. The token rides as a `?token=<token>` query parameter on the webhook POST (webhooks.config:23: `url = http://review-bot:8000/webhook?token=__BOT_TOKEN__`) AND — crossing the internet boundary — on the PUBLIC operator `/review/rerun?token=$TOKEN` path over `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/...` (runbooks/review-bot-ops.md). Query strings are a classic secret-in-logs sink (nginx access log, receiver request log, Gerrit webhooks-plugin delivery log). The plan bounds the token's exposure in `refs/meta/config` but does NOT state that `?token=` is redacted on the log path — and no log config is pinned anywhere (`search infra/ "access_log"` -> ZERO matches), so the nginx `/review/` location falls back to the default combined log capturing the token for the public `/rerun` path. This is overlay check (d): a plaintext secret exposed via logging.
  - criteria: [preview]
  - location: Round-11 remediation -> inbound-auth note; infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23; infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md; infra/nginx/ (`/review/` location)
  - evidence: code fact — the token is in the URL query on both the internal webhook and the PUBLIC `/rerun` path; ABSENCE of any log-redaction statement for `?token=` (`search infra/ "access_log"` -> 0 hits); overlay check (d) secret lifecycle / no secret-logging.
  - suggested_fix: State the redaction posture in S4a — e.g. nginx `/review/` logs a redacted URI (map to strip `token=...`, or `access_log off` on that location) and the receiver masks the token in its request log; OR move the token to an HTTP header if the plugin/receiver supports one, keeping only the network-ACL primary gate in the URL. The redaction is load-bearing for the PUBLIC `/rerun` path since that request crosses the internet boundary.
